title: LLM 时代的个性化推荐：Spotify 生成式推荐引擎的三大技术支柱
author: AI Engineer
content:
Personalization in the Era of LLMs: Inside Spotify's Generative Recommendation Engine

At a recent AI Engineer conference, Shivam Verma, Tech Lead of the User Representations team in Spotify's AI Foundation architecture, provided an explicit, technical breakdown of how the streaming giant is revamping its recommendation engine. Managing a catalog of over 100 million tracks, millions of podcasts, and 400,000 audiobooks for over 750 million monthly active users (MAU) requires powerful personalization frameworks. Spotify is stepping beyond classic candidate selection workflows and embracing unified, generative sequential models powered by transformer backbones.

Moving Away from 'Trad-Rec' Frameworks

Historically, modern recommenders relied on what Verma labels Trad-Rec (Traditional Recommendation) infrastructures. This framework involves multi-step pipelines where a massive catalog is progressively filtered down:

  1. Candidate Generation: Millions of catalog items are filtered down to a few hundred candidates.
  2. Ranking Stages: Independent product rankers filter items further to output localized choices (e.g., specific algorithms for home shelves, search bars, ads, or podcast surfaces).

This classic design creates organizational silos where independent teams maintain distinct models. Spotify is pivoting away from this distributed method toward a single unified transformer model that treats historical user interactions, product channels, and queries as inputs to a central backbone, mirroring modern Large Language Models.

The Three Pillars of LLM-Driven Personalization

Spotify's next-generation architecture centers on three technical core pillars:

┌─────────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Foundational User Model    │     │    Catalog Understanding    │
│ (Embeddings via Hypersphere)│     │  (Hierarchical Semantic IDs)│
└──────────────┬──────────────┘     └──────────────┬──────────────┘
               │                                   │
               └─────────────────┬─────────────────┘
                                 ▼
                    ┌─────────────────────────────┐
                    │ Steerable Generative System │
                    │     (Soft Tokenization)     │
                    └─────────────────────────────┘

1. Foundational User Modeling

To accurately grasp historical taste, Spotify records user interactions inside an embedding space. Verma's team previously deployed autoencoder compression-decompression models to map out vectors.

Today, they feed interaction histories sequentially through custom transformer layers. This architecture achieves multi-modal cross-content modeling, placing music tracks (visualized as clusters), podcast episodes, and users on a shared hypersphere embedding space. This positioning ensures that a data engineer's embedding maps tightly alongside niche tech podcasts, facilitating contextual mathematical lookups.

2. Catalog Understanding via Semantic IDs

Integrating proprietary platform data with open-weight foundation models (such as Llama or Qwen) presents formatting challenges. LLMs process text tokens, whereas recommenders use high-dimensional item vectors. Spotify solves this translation issue by implementing Semantic IDs.

Originally pioneered by Google for YouTube, this method tokenizes a dense 1000-dimensional content vector into 4 to 6 discrete string tokens. These tokens maintain a hierarchical nature:

  • Mainstream pop artists like Ariana Grande and Bruno Mars share identical leading token sequences because of genre commonalities.
  • Terminal tokens diverge to capture unique stylistic niches.

This compression allows fine-tuned LLMs to process platform items autoregressively, calculating the likelihood of a specific track or episode following a user's recent history.

3. Assembling Steerable Pipelines with Soft Tokenization

Training an LLM on 750 million independent user profiles is computationally unfeasible. Collaborative filtering handles general patterns, but real-time personalization requires raw vector injects. Spotify solves this via Soft Tokenization.

During live execution, a user's underlying taste vector is mathematically projected directly into the continuous token space of the target LLM. This injection outputs a dynamic context token ('soft token') integrated directly inside the text prompt. When the system executes a prompt, it preserves deep individual context, generating customized, accurate recommendations.

Production Performance and User Steerability

This generative engine powers Spotify's modern user-facing applications. Features like the AI DJ, prompted playlist generation, and newly extended podcast search capabilities leverage these unified sequence transformations.

Furthermore, this architecture powers the roll-out of interactive Taste Profiles. This mechanism pulls back the curtain on internal model inferences, showing individuals what the platform assumes about their interests and providing granular, natural language controls to delete or highlight specific habits. Internal evaluations confirm positive impacts across core production metrics, proving that generative transformer architectures are robust, efficient alternatives to old-school ranking systems.
aiSummary: Spotify 技术负责人详解如何以统一 LLM 驱动的生成式推荐引擎取代碎片化的传统推荐流水线，核心支撑三大支柱：用户基础嵌入、语义 ID 目录分词与软分词实时个性化。
oneSentenceSummary: Spotify 技术负责人详解如何以统一 LLM 驱动的生成式推荐引擎取代碎片化的传统推荐流水线，核心支撑三大支柱：用户基础嵌入、语义 ID 目录分词与软分词实时个性化。
summary: Shivam Verma 是 Spotify AI Foundation 团队用户表征组的技术负责人，他以第一视角完整讲解了 Spotify 如何为全球 7.5 亿月活用户现代化推荐基础设施。演讲围绕三大核心支柱展开。第一，用户基础建模：Spotify 从基于自编码器的用户嵌入迁移至以 Transformer 为骨干的序列模型，将用户、歌曲和播客节目映射到同一超球面嵌入空间，实现十亿用户规模下的跨内容建模。第二，通过语义 ID 理解内容目录：将 1000 维内容向量压缩为 4-6 个离散层次化 Token，让在 Spotify 数据上微调的开源 LLM（Llama、Qwen）能够自回归地预测用户下一首播放的歌曲或节目。层次化 Token 结构以 Ariana Grande 和 Bruno Mars 为例，两位艺术家作为流行歌手共享前导 Token，而在末级 Token 处产生分叉。第三，通过软分词构建可调控的流水线：由于对全部 7.5 亿用户分别训练模型不可行，系统将用户品味向量以数学方式投影到 LLM 的连续 Token 空间，形成动态「软 Token」注入生成路径，实现实时个性化。该架构已驱动多个生产功能，包括 AI DJ、提示词驱动的播放列表生成（已扩展至播客），以及新发布的「Taste Profile」。Taste Profile 让用户查看 Spotify 对其品味的推断结果，并通过自然语言进行编辑。内部指标确认了正向的生产效果，标志着这一架构已成功替代传统孤岛式排序范式。

mainPoints.point: 从孤岛式传统推荐流水线迁移至单一统一 Transformer 模型
mainPoints.explanation: 传统推荐系统采用多步骤流水线，候选召回与排序由独立团队分别维护。Spotify 将这些环节整合为单一 Transformer 骨干模型，类似 LLM 的工作方式，同时处理用户历史、产品场景和查询请求，消除了组织壁垒和特征覆盖不一致的问题。

mainPoints.point: 语义 ID 将高维内容向量压缩为 LLM 兼容的离散 Token
mainPoints.explanation: 1000 维内容向量被分词为 4-6 个层次化离散 Token，使开源 LLM 能够以自回归方式学习平台内容目录的上下文。层次化结构意味着风格相近的艺术家共享前导 Token，末级 Token 则捕捉细粒度风格差异，让模型预测下一首歌曲或节目就如同预测下一个词。

mainPoints.point: 软分词将用户嵌入直接投影到 LLM Token 空间，实现实时个性化
mainPoints.explanation: 在 7.5 亿用户规模下为每个用户单独训练模型不可行。为此，系统将预构建的用户嵌入向量以数学方式投影到 LLM 的连续 Token 空间，生成动态「软 Token」并注入 Prompt，无需重新训练即可让模型的生成路径反映具体用户的收听历史。

mainPoints.point: 跨内容超球面嵌入空间实现多模态统一用户理解
mainPoints.explanation: 基于 Transformer 的用户模型将用户、音乐曲目与播客节目置于同一超球面嵌入空间，使系统能够跨模态召回语义连贯的推荐内容——例如，机器学习工程师的嵌入向量会聚集在科技播客附近——并支持在用户嵌入邻域内探索发现。

mainPoints.point: Taste Profile 向用户开放模型推断结果，并提供自然语言调控入口
mainPoints.explanation: Spotify 新上线的 Taste Profile 让用户查看平台对其兴趣偏好的推断，并通过自然语言消息进行编辑，例如希望多听某位艺术家的作品或降低某类播客的权重。这些编辑结果会反馈到生成模型，形成用户意图与推荐质量之间的闭环。

keyQuotes: 这与传统智能体意义上的上下文工程有所不同，我们更关注如何在建模层面做上下文工程。
我们正在从孤岛式的工作模式转向单一统一模型，这与 LLM 的工作方式类似。
语义 ID 是一个相当新颖的概念，我认为几年前 Google 在 YouTube 场景中率先提出了这一思路。
你将用户表征投影到 LLM 的 Token 空间，这样就能在模型内部创建所谓的软 Token。
我们有表征用户的嵌入，有表征内容压缩版本的语义 ID，还有这种软分词方法，让你能把用户投影到模型的 Token 空间。
